{"title":"Security Deposit Demand Letter Service | DepositBack","meta_description":"Need your security deposit back? Create a state-specific demand letter in minutes. Legally force your landlord to return what's yours. Start now.","content_outline":["The Silent Epidemic: When Landlords Keep Your Deposit","Why a Security Deposit Demand Letter Is Your Strongest First Move","State-by-State Deadlines: Know What Your Landlord Actually Owes","How DepositBack Works: From Template to Threat in 5 Minutes","Next Steps: Small Claims Court and Statutory Damages"],"key_points":["Landlords in most states have 14 to 30 days to return a deposit or provide an itemized deduction list.","A professionally written demand letter creates a court-admissible paper trail and proves you know your rights.","DepositBack auto-fills state-specific legal language so renters can send a compliant letter without hiring a lawyer."],"cta":"Generate My Demand Letter Now - It's Free","target_audience":"US renters whose landlords have failed to return a security deposit within state-mandated deadlines and need a legally compliant demand letter to recover their funds.","word_count_target":800,"email_sequence":[{"email_number":1,"type":"urgency_education","subject_line":"Your landlord kept your deposit. Here's your 48-hour plan.","preview_text":"Stop waiting. A security deposit demand letter is the fastest way to force a response.","body_copy":"Hi [First Name],

Your lease ended weeks ago. Your landlord still has your money.

In most states, that is illegal. If they have missed the deadline, you do not need a lawyer-you need a paper trail.

A security deposit demand letter tells your landlord you know your rights and you are ready to escalate. Most landlords pay up immediately once they see it is serious.

DepositBack builds your letter in under 5 minutes, with state-specific language that holds up in court.

[CTA: Start My Letter Now]

Do not let them keep what is yours.

The DepositBack Team"},{"email_number":2,"type":"social_proof_template","subject_line":"Re: [State] law gives you the upper hand","preview_text":"Templates are nice. State law is better. Use both.","body_copy":"Hi [First Name],

Still waiting on your deposit? You are not alone-but you are running out of time.

Every day you wait, your landlord assumes you have given up. That changes now.

Our security deposit demand letter template is not generic. It is built for your state, cites the actual statute your landlord violated, and sets a hard
